issues.title: Typing with Pinyin-Simplified Input Source while selecting text, the navigation box is positioned incorrectly
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Steps to reproduce 1. Use macOS 2. Copy a large text and paste it into the TextField 3. Select a few lines of text 4. Switch Input Source to Pinyin - Simplified 5. Type nihao See the attached video and screenshots. ### Expected results The Input Source navigation box should be below the typing text. ### Actual results Wrong position. ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple), useMaterial3: true, ), home: Scaffold( appBar: AppBar( backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.inversePrimary, title: const Text('Title'), ), body: const Center( child: TextField( maxLines: null, ), ), ), ); } } ```

This looks like it's using the final index of the selection area instead of the first index.

Got a fix out for this in #137863; as we suspected we were using the selection base offset rather than the selection start.

Thanks for filing this @fanpei91.
This is now fixed on `master` branch.
This bug is not fixed. Please reopen this issue.

Hi @fanpei91. You have to switch to the latest `master` channel 3.17.0-1.0.pre.60 to see this. I can see this has been fixed there.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/53122008/a7e4630c-e6ab-4dc4-8731-411d520fbec1

@fanpei91 nice catch - you're correct. 

My patch fixed the horizontal positioning for the bulk of scenarios but in the case where editing the text causes the size/position of the textfield itself to be changed and thereby causes the cursor to be displaced, the position will still be incorrect.

A fix for this is relatively straightforward but likely to get lower priority since affects a pretty small subset of cases, and moving the entire widget around as the user types is a much narrower subset of cases than the usual case of relatively static textfields.

The even more extreme case where the user is doing text input in a textfield that is being animated around the screen is something I suspect we would not work on.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

Thanks fo filing the isue

@jmagman I believe this is an issue where the native text field contents are not being cleared in response to clearing the field via dictation. This specific text input plugin call is `TextInput.clearClient`.

When the text is cleared via the controller clear button, `-[FlutterTextInputView setTextInputState:]` is correctly setting the `FlutterTextInputView.text` to an empty string.  However, when it calls  `[self.inputDelegate textWillChange:self]`

https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/cb82d5e7b8a94fb6bd5c8ca681361c190e31a1ed/shell/platform/darwin/ios/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm#L662-L663
```obj-c
  if (textChanged) {
    [self.inputDelegate textWillChange:self];
```

 the `UIDictationController` is jumping in to flush the serialized dictation results, which calls `-[FlutterTextInputView insertText:@"Hello"]`. So the text state halfway through the `setTextInputState` gets out of sync:

```
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = breakpoint 8.1
    frame #0: 0x000000010046afc0 Flutter`-[FlutterTextInputView insertText:](self=Failed to get the 'some' field from optional 'self', _cmd="insertText:", text="Hello") at FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm:1245:3
    frame #1: 0x00000001918b9860 UIKitCore`-[UIDictationController insertSerializedDictationResult:withCorrectionIdentifier:] + 820
    frame #2: 0x00000001c3f536b0 UIKit`-[UIDictationControllerAccessibility insertSerializedDictationResult:withCorrectionIdentifier:] + 720
    frame #3: 0x00000001918b4140 UIKitCore`__55-[UIDictationController _setupHypothesisAsFinalResults]_block_invoke + 196
    frame #4: 0x00000001918b9164 UIKitCore`-[UIDictationController performIgnoringDocumentChanges:] + 40
    frame #5: 0x00000001918b41c4 UIKitCore`-[UIDictationController _runFinalizeOperation:] + 40
    frame #6: 0x00000001918b4454 UIKitCore`-[UIDictationController setState:] + 432
    frame #7: 0x00000001918b8978 UIKitCore`-[UIDictationController cancelDictation] + 1520
    frame #8: 0x00000001c3f53358 UIKit`-[UIDictationControllerAccessibility cancelDictation] + 40
    frame #9: 0x0000000191928e64 UIKitCore`-[UIKeyboardImpl prepareForSelectionChange] + 472
    frame #10: 0x0000000191d7a7dc UIKitCore`-[UITextInteractionInputDelegate textWillChange:] + 180
  * frame #11: 0x00000001004680cc Flutter`-[FlutterTextInputView setTextInputState:](self=Failed to get the 'some' field from optional 'self', _cmd="setTextInputState:", state=7 key/value pairs) at FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm:663:5
```

There doesn't seem to be a programatic way to dismiss the dictation prompt (or even to detect that it started), which is what the search bar does when you tap the cancel button.  `AssistantServices.framework` on `assistantd` is private. `-insertDictationResult:` happens too late, same time as `insertText:`.  There's no way to get the dictation state off of a `UITextInputCurrentInputModeDidChangeNotification` notification.  Maybe the text input needs a hidden search bar to initiate the cancel action when the controller is cleared?  Seems ugly though.

I have exactly the same issue when I call `_textController.clear()` while dictating text. But I've noticed that after I call `_textController.clear()` once the dictation mode is disabled and the text stays as it was in the text field but when I start typing on the keyboard the text is cleared and only the characters I type are now visible.

Hi @leoshusar, Thanks for filing the issue. 

I am having hard time understanding the reproducible steps, Could you please clarify it, specifically this part.

> When I paste some text with CRLF endings into a TextField and click on the end of the line, 

How do you paste the CRLF character in the texfield? and whats the expected vs actual results it would be great if you could update the main post.

Hi @maheshmnj, I have updated the main post. I hope it's more understandable now.

Thanks for the update @leoshusar, I can reproduce the same behavior as you

Steps to reproduce
- Run the first code sample from the main Post.
- Paste the CRLF text in the textfield from the `crlf.txt` file in the main post.
- Clicking at the end of a line with a mouse the cursor is shown after a CRLF character (with a visible space between text and cursor)
 - On pressing the left arrow key the cursor moves to the end of text and pressing the right arrow key the cursor moves to next line as if there was no character at the end. 
 
This happens on both MacOS & Windows Desktop and not on the web, This can be understood from the below video comparison on Desktop and Web. Reproducible on both stable and the master channel.

issues.title: [Windows]TextFormField on Desktop is missing the alternate Shortcuts for copy, cut, paste.
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

Create a Windows Desktop app.
Either copy my below code or add a TextFormField.
Start the app.
Click the TextFormField to begin editing.

Type this text:  Flutter Rocks

Attempt to use these below Shortcuts to copy, cut, and paste text.

`CTRL + Insert` - copies the selected text into the clipboard.
`SHIFT + Insert` - pastes the clipboard text into the text field at the insertion point.
`SHIFT + Delete` - cuts the currently selected text, and copies it to the clipboard.

**Expected results:** 
Windows users expect these shortcuts to work when editing text.

Thank you for adding these to the SDK and have a great day!!

**Actual results:** 
Nothing happens.

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>



```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const HomePage(),
    );
  }
}
```

@maheshmnj please forgive me, but I forgot `CTRL + Insert`

**These three work together on Windows, and should for all the desktop platforms.**  

`CTRL + Insert` - copies the selected text into the clipboard.
`SHIFT + Insert` - pastes the clipboard text into the text field at the insertion point.
`SHIFT + Delete` - cuts the currently selected text, and copies it to the clipboard.

Some might push back on macOS, but the number of Windows users that want their Mac keyboard to work like Windows is, well a lot, LOL.  You can Google, [how to make my mac keyboard shortcuts like windows](https://www.google.com/search?q=how+to+make+my+mac+keyboard+shortcuts+like+windows&oq=how+to+make+my+mac+keyboard+shortcuts+like+windows&aqs=chrome..69i57.13295j0j7&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8), lots of users want this.

![20200728_141801](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3411015/88660233-0649b680-d0df-11ea-8525-1671743e8fcf.gif)


1. Call showDatePicker method and choose edit mode in dialog. (Tested with 'tr' locale)
2. Try to change the TextField manually. It changes from dot(.) format to slashes(/). Then when you enter, you get invalid format error. If you delete them all, then you can only enter digits because you don't have control over keyboard.



**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
What I expect from this feature(DatePickerEntryMode.input) is to be able to disable/enable it. If I can't disable it, I want to control it. I want to be able to specify date format, control keyboard etc.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
As specified at step 2, when you try to change the TextField manually, It changes from dot(.) format to slashes(/). Then when you enter, you get invalid format error. If you delete them all, then you can only enter digits because you don't have control over keyboard.

hI @figengungor 
Just reopened this issue https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/55535, it would have been fixed but i can reproduce 
Please follow up on that issue, I'm closing the current one as a duplicate.
If you disagree, please write in the comments and I will reopen it.
Thank you

Hey @TahaTesser Can you open this issue again, please? 
Leaving the reason here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/63372#issuecomment-680190908

I see where the confusion was. There are two related issues here:

- The handling of the separators was broken when the translations were not in place. This was fixed with #63461.
- The keyboard that is coming up on certain devices does not include separator characters needed to enter a date.

I will reopen this bug to address the keyboard issue.

It would appear to be a problem with specific devices, as I don't see the problem on an Android Emulator (API 30):

![DateKeyboard](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19588/91370127-6147f980-e7c2-11ea-8052-41023b44c89f.png)

Can you tell us what devices you are seeing this problem on?

We are requesting the [InputType.TYPE_CLASS_DATETIME](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/text/InputType#TYPE_CLASS_DATETIME) keyboard which one would think would include separators needed for entering a date and time for a given locale, but it appears that some devices just show the number entry keyboard.

Couple of possible ways to address this:

- Don't specify the type of keyboard and just let the system bring up the default keyboard.

  This is a the safest, but leads to a less than great user experience for most users, as most devices bring up a date time keyboard designed for date entry.

- Special case certain devices that we know have this issue and just use the default keyboard for those.

  This is problematic in that we would be continually tracking these issues. And I think that it may be specific to whatever keyboard IME support the user has set up on their device.

- Expose the `keyboardType` parameter in the `showDatePicker/showDateRangePicker()`, and `InputDatePickerFormField` that would allow apps to override with what ever keyboard request they want.

  We could do this independent of the the first two. It would allow the apps to override the default, but it would be nice if they didn't have to.

Hi,

I'm having the same problem, the emulator works correctly, but on my phone the date (/) separator does not appear.

Location pt - BR.

Phone - Samsung Galaxy S20.

It certainly looks like this is mostly a problem with Samsung devices. However, given that it is apparently up to the keyboard installed by the user to decide what it wants to show for a `InputType.TYPE_CLASS_DATETIME` keyboard, it would probably be best to err on the conservative side and go with the full default keyboard for the date pickers and expose the `keyboardType` as a parameter so that apps can override this if they want.

Opinions/feedback on this welcome.
I tested on Lg G5. This feature cannot be used on certain devices so in order to make it functional, the keyboardType can be exposed for now. We can think of better ways to do it later.
Do we have any info on the solution finalized and date by which this would be fixed, also any other workarounds for this till we have a fix?

I agree with @figengungor in that exposing the keyboardType would be a suitable workaround for this in the short term.

I am perhaps not skilled enough to submit a pull request but I would suggest the following changes to: `packages/flutter/lib/src/material/pickers/input_date_picker.dart` for consideration:

- Adding a `keyboardType` variable:

```
/// If provided, it will specify the keyboard type used. If this is
/// null, it will default to TextInputType.datetime.
final TextInputType? keyboardType;
```

- Add `keyboardType` as an argument to `InputDatePickerFormField` e.g. `this.keyboardType`

- Inside the `TextFormField()`, change the `keyboardType` parameter to:

```
widget.keyboardType ?? TextInputType.datetime
```

This would keep `TextInputType.datetime` as the default value for `keyboardType`.

Then the `keyboardType` value could just be passed up the widget tree until it appears as an argument for `showDatePicker()`.

Hi. I've stumbled into this issue. There is no **/:** button in the keyboard when I try to edit the date from the DatePicker through the edit/pencil icon. Are there any ways to show the correct keyboard, or any workaround?
Same probleme here!
Any solutions?

Push. This issue is of at least moderate importance. Right now the only solution to enable all users an equal expierence, is not using the date picker dialog and instead going for a standard text field since only there the keyboard type can be specified to an extent where things work properly. 
We had this kind of issue with the android keyboards earlier in the beginning of 2020 where some implementations would not be correct but since flutter has the aim of being environment agnostic, this issue should also be addressed within flutter.
I think it may help bring this to the Flutter core team's attention if everyone was to upvote the initial post in this issue.

This is a big problem which makes the date picker near to useless in some devices.
This is what I have on a `Samsung Galaxy Note 9 / SM-N960U1` with default Samsung keyboard, no way to add a manual date without separators...

![no_keyboard_separator](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4432213/122024182-7b41df80-cdc8-11eb-8455-6e529dd70955.png)
cc: @HansMuller
This is still a problem for some LG and Samsung keyboards. The same problem apparently exists for native Android APIs -- https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/issues/682

Is not exactly a solution but i used this to dont show the input picker, is an attribute in the showDatePicker method:
     
   initialEntryMode: DatePickerEntryMode.calendarOnly,
Have the same issue. Allowing to customize keyboarType and inputFormatters for InputDatePickerFormField will be a good solution, I suppose.
I opened a PR to allow overriding keyboardType in showDatePicker, should be a temporary fix for now by using TextInputType.text for example.

Instead of keyboardType, allowing override of inputFormatters, would solve many other problems.
A better aproach would be to override textField at all...

issues.title: TextField onChange method inconsistent when characters are spammed
issues.body:

## What the code sample does
I am trying to build a text editor on top of the TextField. The code sample format's the text within a text field, adding a line break at every 10th character.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Simultaneously press multiple keys on the keyboard just before a line break occurs <!-- describe steps to demonstrate bug -->

**Expected results:** Text formatted correctly with a line break at every 10th character. <!-- what did you want to see? -->

**Actual results:** Sometimes the formatting method within onChange does not apply correctly, meaning a line can exceed 10 characters before a line break. Occasionally some characters typed are deleted. <!-- what did you see? -->

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:google_fonts/google_fonts.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  // This widget is the root of your application.
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(primarySwatch: Colors.blue),
      home: const TestEditor(),
    );
  }
}

class TestEditor extends StatefulWidget {
  const TestEditor({super.key});

  @override
  State<TestEditor> createState() => _TestEditorState();
}

class _TestEditorState extends State<TestEditor> {
  final contoller = TextEditingController();

  void onChange(String text) {
    // Method 1
    if (text.length % 10 == 0 && text.isNotEmpty) {
      final selection = contoller.selection.copyWith();
      contoller.text += '\n';
      contoller.selection = selection.copyWith(
        baseOffset: selection.baseOffset + 1,
        extentOffset: selection.extentOffset + 1,
      );
    }
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: SafeArea(
        child: SizedBox(
          width: MediaQuery.of(context).size.width,
          height: MediaQuery.of(context).size.height,
          child: TextField(
            style: GoogleFonts.robotoMono(),
            cursorWidth: 2,
            controller: contoller,
            onChanged: onChange,
            showCursor: true,
            smartQuotesType: SmartQuotesType.disabled,
            smartDashesType: SmartDashesType.disabled,
            enableSuggestions: false,
            autocorrect: false,
            maxLines: 500,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

comments: Thanks for the report @ChunKaiC After running your code and inputing random text in the textfield, I see that first line gets 10 chars but other lines shows 9 chars as below: Is that the behavior you are seeing and reporting ? Or you see another different result ?

Yes, my mistake, the screen shot you sent is the correct behaviour. The problem I'm currently experiencing is that sometimes when you spam right before a line break is about to be inserted this happens:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/70914855/206458824-aff4c326-704b-4ecc-a8e3-d4e63e052f0a.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/70914855/206458968-c7ab1569-5a30-477e-bce9-81e1e784fd8a.png)
(This was done by pressing 'd' after the previous screen shot)

This was found on the iOS simulator, however the problem seems to occur on iOS release mode as well.

This is an edge case that occurs whenever you have 2 inputs that have the same autofill hint.  This is because the `name` `id` and `autocomplete` attributes are all informed by that autofill hint passed to the widget.  

We can decouple the setting of the `id` attribute from the autofill hints or append an incremental id to make them unique. However, I wonder if setting the id is even necessary on our end? I see some tests that check for the existence of the `id`, but the same could be accomplished by checking `name`.

If there is a widget tree where there are inputs that have the same autofill hint, isn't that an error?

If so, could we use an assert instead?

Having duplicate autofill hints for inputs on the same page isn't necessarily an error and there are legitimate use cases for it (shipping + billing forms, your example, etc).  We definitely shouldn't be setting the same id on our end though.

issues.title: `SliverMainAxisGroup` causes `showOnScreen` calls from descendants of a pinned `SliverPersistentHeader` to reset the scroll position
comments:
From my own investigation:

- `showOnScreen` call will end up at `RenderViewportBase.showInViewport`
- from there, `getOffsetToReveal` is called for the leading and trailing edges
- inside that, `isPinned` is `false` when we're using `SliverMainAxisGroup`, since the layout function of `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup` doesn't report `maxScrollObstructionExtent`.
- due to this, it calculates that the leading offset of the `TextField` is located at offset 0.0, therefore scrolling the view up to 0.

the simple fix that solves this specific issue is to return the calculated `maxScrollObstructionExtent` in the  `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup`'s geometry, but I'm not sure if there are any other issues that can arise from this (e.g. combinations of different persistent headers, some other crazy layouts, etc).

Thanks for the report. I'm also able to reproduce the issue on the latest Flutter stable and main channels.

Maybe the fix for cases at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167801 (a duplicate issue at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/173333 too) hasn't tested this case yet. Kindly cc @navaronbracke (author of the [fix](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/168450) for https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167801). I also found https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/130259, which was opened quite long time ago, looks similar a bit to this issue.

When I first reported https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167801 I found that it was caused by a "fix" for another issue, hence why I opened the revert commit in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/168450, which included a regression test.

There has since been a proper fix for the original issue, that didn't break that regression test that I added (see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/171339)

So either this is a new bug, or #171339 didn't entirely fix the issue with SliverPersistentHeaders?

I think this is a new bug - #171339 is about slivers **after** a pinned header, this one is about calling `showOnScreen` from  within the pinned header itself (when it's placed in a `SliverMainAxisGroup`). I've outlined my findings and a possible fix in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/177453#issuecomment-3437426231, which works for this specific case, but I'm worried that it might break other layouts

> the simple fix that solves this specific issue is to return the calculated maxScrollObstructionExtent in the RenderSliverMainAxisGroup's geometry, but I'm not sure if there are any other issues that can arise from this (e.g. combinations of different persistent headers, some other crazy layouts, etc).

@yiiim WDYT of this?

> the simple fix that solves this specific issue is to return the calculated `maxScrollObstructionExtent` in the `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup`'s geometry, but I'm not sure if there are any other issues that can arise from this (e.g. combinations of different persistent headers, some other crazy layouts, etc).

If we do this, as long as there is a pinned child in `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup`, the `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup` will be considered as `isPinned`. We will encounter a similar issue: when a `TextField` in a non-pinned child within `RenderSliverMainAxisGroup` gains focus, it will not scroll.

If you can solve this issue, feel free to submit a PR and I will review it.

@kekland: check out PR #178809, it might solve your problem.
@yiiim, could you please have a look? I really need this feature for my project too 😄 . Thanks!

issues.title: TextInputFormatter and Samsung Keyboard Autocorrect give wrong results due to race conditions
comments:
Thanks for the report @Bassiuz 
Does it occur with native Android app on same affected device ? 
If not, can you please upgrade to latest stable (3.32.5) and re-run your scenario to check if the behavior still persist or not ?

Thanks for the update @Bassiuz 
So if I understand correctly, the same behavior also doesn't work with native Android implementation ? If so, it could be specific to the Samsung keyboard + autocorrect. Is there a way for you to try on another android device (different make than Samsung) to narrow down the issue.
For now, instead of `TextCapitalization.none,`, can you check if with `TextCapitalization.words` help ?

The app behaves the same way on Native and in Flutter; and only on Samsung devices. We have Google Pixels and One Plus devices that do not have this problem. So it seems that it has something to do with the delay in autocorrect picked up by the Samsung keyboard.

We currently have set it to TextCapitalization.words as a fallback. However, our case is for names like mine (Bas de Vaan) where you want to capitalize some words, but want to exclude some from capitalization (such as 'de'). So this is not a full solution.

> The app behaves the same way on Native and in Flutter; and only on Samsung devices. We have Google Pixels and One Plus devices that do not have this problem. So it seems that it has something to do with the delay in autocorrect picked up by the Samsung keyboard.

Thanks for the update. I am not sure if this would be actionable from Flutter side, as native also behaves the same and it seems to be an issue with Samsung's autocorrect feature itself and likely need to be reported with Samsung. You may try to clear the cache or reset the keyboard settings to see if it helps further in your case or not.

We see this quite consistently on different Samsung devices; so clearing the cache or resetting the keyboard is not really an option.

Thanks for the help, i'll keep looking for alternatives :).
I'll keep the issue open and label for team's tracking.

issues.title: [web] Virtual Keyboard on Raspberry Pi
issues.body:
I am still struggling with this issue.  I saw this issue that seemed somewhat related:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/32763

So if flutter web is used on an iphone, the virtual keyboard sounds like it will come up?  I'm wondering then how does flutter know when you're in an environment with or without a physical keyboard?  If I'm running in a web browser for example on a raspberry pi, and there is no keyboard plugged in (I haven't gotten hardware to try this yet...) then does the virtual keyboard come up?  

Is there a way to force flutter to think I do NOT have a physical keyboard?  If I test this on my laptop, the flutter web app works great with my physical keyboard, but I'd like to test with virtual and ultimately I want to us it on a pi with a touch screen so that may already work?
comments: Ok, I just confirmed, if I unplug the keyboard on the pi, and just have a usb mouse, the textinput fields do NOT trigger a virtual keyboard in flutter to come up.

Hi @jdeltoft 
I'm aware of this issue about ["not open keyboard on TextField focus" ](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863), 
but if I understand correctly what you want to achieve 
is making Flutter Web unaware of a Physical keyboard attached,
is that correct?
This is certainly something achievable, 
but if your goal is to force a virtual keyboard to show up, 
I'm not sure this is the correct approach.
Out ouf curiosity I've just tested flutter web app with a simple TextField 
on Win10 (touchscreen + keyboard): 
-  **in tablet mode** + **open** :  no virtual keyboard,
-  **in tablet mode** + **folded** :  virtual keyboard comes out;

as expected since the virtual keyboard is provided by the OS and not by Flutter.
Does make Flutter "ignore" keyboard events still match your needs
given that it might not trigger a virtual keyboard?
thank you

@jdeltoft I believe you are correct in your last assertion,
when you run Flutter on a Raspberry Pi with touch screen
will be used the virtual keyboard of the OS installed on the device.
(BTW I have a Yoga 720, not Surface Pro :) )
What OS is installed on your Raspberry Pi?
thank you

@iapicca I'm using raspbian OS on the pi, but we're launching it into "kiosk" mode where chrome launches and you see the web app.  The concern I have with the virtual keyboards I've seen on raspbian is that they look like "x windows" type of keyboards.  So those aren't probably going to show well or at all in my case.  

I think what I'll have to do is try something like this:

https://pub.dev/packages/virtual_keyboard

and launch this only when I see that a text field has focus.  Not sure how feasible/viable that is to detect focus changes.  Looks like I have some homework.  :)

I will try the raspbian matchbox keyboard though just to see if it works.
We're not setup to test on Raspberry Pi, unfortunately. However, perhaps @nturgut has some ideas for you to try.

This is a really crucial need that opens up a new dimension of IOT possibilities. We can build  Kiosk applications like Mc Donald's Self-ordering app using a low-cost device like Raspberry Pi with Flutter Web. That kind of application needs a virtual keyboard so that the users feel like using a native mobile app. Looking forward to this.
CC @eseidelGoogle

Perhaps someone with a Raspberry Pi is willing to attempt a fix? Web-specific text editing logic lives in this file: https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/master/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart

Searching for `browserEngine` will reveal various browser-specific hacks we had to put in place for things to work uniformly across all browsers.

I think we may have our wires crossed on my original question and the need.   Sorry for this, but I'm also learning as I go on this one.  So my initial (wrong) thought was that the keyboard was coming up from flutter when I'm on Android or iOS.  But of course it's those OS's that are showing the virtual keyboard when you have a text entry widget with focus.  

I think part of the confusion also comes in here from the fact that when using Safari on iOS, there was some other issues with the iOS virtual keyboard and Flutter?  I'm not yet clear on this, but assume it was when trying to use flutter web pages in safari on iOS?  

Anyway, in my ideal world, flutter would have it's own virtual keyboard that you could use either instead of the OS one, or as a keyboard when you don't have one.  In my case on Raspberry Pi in Kiosk (touch screen) mode where the OS is Raspbian (Debian on pi) and the browser is Chrome, there is no virtual keyboard that ever shows up.  If I have a usb keyboard on the pi, that works, but I want to just have a touch screen.  

Some things I'm wondering if they could work:

* Pure flutter solution: https://pub.dev/packages/virtual_keyboard
* Possible flutter solution: https://pub.dev/packages/keyboard_actions (@diegoveloper maybe you could comment?
* JS solution: https://github.com/hodgef/simple-keyboard

I have NO idea yea how to add a keyboard like that last JS one.  The point of me doing flutter was partly because I've never developed in JS.  I'm an embedded C/C++ firmware developer mainly.  :)
We currently do not have any plans to implement a keyboard in Flutter. Maybe somebody from the community would be interested in implementing a keyboard widget for use cases like this?

> We currently do not have any plans to implement a keyboard in Flutter. Maybe somebody from the community would be interested in implementing a keyboard widget for use cases like this?

Thanks @goderbauer for the reply, as mentioned I'll try to see if I hit any blockers using that package: https://pub.dev/packages/virtual_keyboard

Just as and FYI, this is working out well for me.  One thing others might be interested in if they come across this same ticket.  I am using focus nodes to know where the cursor is, and then I wanted to debug sometimes on an actual android device so I wanted to prevent the soft keyboard from ever coming up.  

This solves that.  Use the "readonly" parameter to the TextFormField.  

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/16863#issuecomment-522745415

@goderbauer Sorry if my question was unclear. I didn't mean that we bundle an implementation in the framework. What I meant was whether we want `EditableText` to allow implementations that are not tied to a built-in method channel. It is entirely possible that such a thing is already possible, and that I'm simply not aware of it.

issues.title: Text selection handles are missplaced on any rotated text
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

- add any kind of selection widget (for example a `SelectionArea`)
- add some widgets below that contains text
- you can see the text selection handles being correctly placed on any straight text
- you'll see the handles getting missplaced when this text is rotated

### Expected results

Handles are correctly placed, as the highlight is

### Actual results

The handles are missplaced

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() => runApp(const MaterialApp(home: Sample()));

class Sample extends StatelessWidget {
  const Sample({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: SelectionArea(
        selectionControls: cupertinoTextSelectionControls,
        child: const Stack(
          children: [
            SampleTextBox('This is a normal text', position: Offset(100, 100), angle: 0),
            SampleTextBox('This is a rotated text', position: Offset(50, 200), angle: 20),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class SampleTextBox extends StatelessWidget {
  const SampleTextBox(
    this.text, {
    super.key,
    required this.position,
    required this.angle,
  });

  final String text;
  final Offset position;
  final double angle;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Positioned(
      top: position.dy,
      left: position.dx,
      child: Transform.rotate(
        angle: angle,
        child: Container(
          color: Colors.green.withOpacity(0.5),
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
          child: Text(text, style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.white)),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

issues.title: SliverAppBar: Copy/Paste controls for TextField are enlarged when AppBar is expanded.
comments:
Definitely an issue

![Screenshot_1584085116](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48603081/76600165-62df7700-652c-11ea-82d7-a1eecb451077.png)
/cc @justinmc

I can reproduce this with the code above.  The reason is that we're using a Transform widget to scale the app bar, and the text selection menu gets scaled too (see [here](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/e6b0f5f238d7c67a686e54d09c91e654fa8f215d/packages/flutter/lib/src/material/flexible_space_bar.dart#L385)).

Should scaling a TextField with a Transform widget also scale the text selection menu?  My instinct is no.  Maybe it would be possible for us to prevent anything in an Overlay from being affected by Transform?

*Update:* Widgets in an Overlay are not affected by Transforms outside of the Overlay. In this case, the text selection menu uses CompositedTransformFollower, which looks up the transform of its target and applies it to its child.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/3579f1c33edfe739a687668d78c9fc10ac9d9567/packages/flutter/lib/src/rendering/layer.dart#L2280

I would think definitely not. The text selection should counter scale its parents value, so as to always remain the same size, assuming it cant be placed in its own overlay, independent of the one the textfield is using (this seems like the 'right' approach imo)

Issue replicable on latest Stable (1.20.2).

![52526](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67046386/91174270-b02b5b80-e6fc-11ea-957a-5be5c991728e.png)

This is tricky. I think what would need to be done to get this working is to create a new FollowerLayer to be used by the text selection menu inside of a CompositedTransformFollower.  Rather than perfectly matching the CompositedTransformTarget, it would need to only match the target's position, while not modifying the follower's size.

That's if we are making the following assumption: A text selection menu should not be scaled by a Transform.  There may be other ways to work around this if we take another approach...

On this topic, this use case of following position and not size has many other general purpose use cases as well. 

For example, trying to make a "selection box" around an object inside interactive viewer, like Figma or Adobe XD would have. The handles of the box should be 10px, the lines always 1px, regardless of the child's zoom level. 

Currently this is impossible with CompositedTransformFollower since it forces the followers scale to match the target. In this case we want to match the size of the child, but not via scaling, via true sizing.

Verified this issue using latest versions and confirmed that the issue persists. Also, this doesn't seem to be specific to Android, but also occurs on iOS.

issues.title: When secure keyboard is enabled, obscure text on TextField requires two clicks to show secure keyboard
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. set obscureText to true
2. Tap on TextField once
3. TextFeild get focus but the keyboard does not pop up

Xiaomi HyperOs has secure keyboard enabled by default.
If I close the system secure keyboard, everything is ok!!

### Expected results

Click on the input box to pop up the secure keyboard

### Actual results

When the TextField component sets obscureText to true, a single click on the TextField will only give it focus without popping up the keyboard. The keyboard will only pop up normally when you click it a second time.

```dart
Column(
```

comments: Hi @coderm4ster, can you share the steps to install `onboard`? I installed it with apt but it keeps crashing with segfault for me so I can't test this properly

Hi @danagbemava-nc!

I also installed onboard using apt, and I haven't encountered any segfault, even after installing it on multiple machines. You could try installing the following additional packages: `at-spi2-core`, `mousetweaks`, and `python3-pyatspi`.

If the issue persists, could you provide details about the operating system and graphical environment you're using? Thanks!

Hi @coderm4ster, I still get the segfault after installing the additional packages. I typically get the segfault when I interact with any of the keys. 

```
07:12:59.545 WARNING HardwareSensorTracker: Failed to connect to acpid, SW_TABLET_MODE detection disabled. ('/var/run/acpid.socket': [Errno 2] No such file or directory) 
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

![Screenshot from 2024-12-11 07-16-15](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b378f2e-7fad-46f2-b616-2c4421bb9925)

Hi @danagbemava-nc!

Thank you! It seems like your system might be missing the `acpid` package. I suggest installing it first and testing again.
If you still encounter a segmentation fault after this, please let me know, and I’ll look further into the issue. One noticeable difference is that you're using Wayland as your graphical subsystem, while I’m on Xorg, but I don't believe this should be the root cause of the problem.

Hi @coderm4ster, I have installed the acpid package and I no longer see that error but I do get the segfault after interacting with the keys on the keyboard. 

```
daniel@daniel-XPS-13-7390:~/nevercode/keyboard_issue$ onboard
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

Hi @danagbemava-nc!

Thank you! If I understand correctly, the keyboard appears, but pressing any key on it triggers a segmentation fault, right? I’ll look into this issue and get back to you as soon as I have more information.
Hi @coderm4ster, yes, that is exactly what happens

Hi @danagbemava-nc!

I installed the same operating system as yours in a virtual machine, but I couldn’t reproduce the segmentation fault. While investigating, I found a bug report that might be related to your issue:

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/onboard/+bug/2063041](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/onboard/+bug/2063041)

In comment 12, they suggest a potential fix:
1. Run the `onboard-settings` command in the terminal.
2. In the Onboard Preferences window, navigate to **Keyboard → Advanced → Input Options**.
3. Change the value of **Input event source** from **XInput** to **GTK**.

This should resolve the issue.
It seems plausible since, on my system, this setting is automatically switched due to a warning:
`18:23:20.418 WARNING TouchInput: XInput event source failed to initialize, falling back to GTK.`

Could you try applying this setting on your system?

Additionally, make sure to enable the **Auto-show when editing text** option under **Auto-show**.
